summary: Apply owner amendment A-10: replace the Windows public-beta eight-hour N-2 reliability soak with a release-eligible one-hour soak while retaining the cadence, midpoint crash recovery, exact memory accounting, and 1.5x growth ceiling.

decisions.topic: Reduce the literal Windows beta reliability soak to one hour
decisions.decision: Supersede D-0399's 28,800-second literal contract with a 3,600-second release contract using a 30-second cadence, 300-second warmup, 300-900-second memory baseline, one verified engine kill at 1,800 seconds, at least 113 successful cycles, 60-second memory samples, the unchanged 1.5x growth ceiling, and the distinct N-2-LITERAL-1-HOUR evidence identity.
decisions.rationale: The owner explicitly reduced the beta gate for a hobbyist-oriented product. Keeping the prior 900-of-960 nominal success ratio requires ceil(0.9375 x 120) = 113 successful cycles in one hour. A new label and event identity prevent historical eight-hour evidence or duration-only accelerated diagnostics from being misclassified as current release evidence.

testEvidence.criterion: Only the exact one-hour literal configuration is release-eligible; accelerated or historical eight-hour identities remain ineligible, and emitter, verifier, PowerShell wrapper, documentation, and spec agree.
testEvidence.test: Change the focused tests first, observe them fail against the old eight-hour constants and event contract, then implement the one-hour contract and rerun the same bounded test lane once.

red.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/scripts/n2-soak-evidence.test.ts tests/scripts/n2-soak-runner.test.ts --reporter=dot --maxWorkers=1
red.outcome: RED: 2 files failed with 3 failed and 10 passed tests. Failures showed the old N-2-LITERAL-8-HOUR label, 28,800-second duration, 14,400-second crash point, eight-hour minimum-duration error, and old PowerShell contract.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/scripts/n2-soak-evidence.test.ts tests/scripts/n2-soak-runner.test.ts --reporter=dot --maxWorkers=1
green.outcome: GREEN: 2 files and all 13 tests passed in 1.18 seconds. The tests bind every literal timing and memory parameter, reject the historical label, prove a valid 3,600-second accelerated configuration remains non-release, prove the 113-cycle boundary and 90-second crash/recovery boundaries, bind the numeric timeout guard, and require the renamed emitter/verifier event.

nearMisses.confirmation: One hour equals the accelerated lane's maximum duration, so duration alone cannot establish release eligibility. Mode, exact configuration, eligibility flag, label, event name, manifest hashes, and verifier checks remain mandatory.

nearMisses.sought: Correction of the old eight-hour decision
nearMisses.confirmation: The historical provenance entry is immutable. This new entry explicitly supersedes D-0399 instead of rewriting earlier evidence.

questions: A-10 is resolved by the owner's direct instruction. Q-0038 and Q-0039 remain the only owner decisions blocking public release.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
